title: Best Actor Goes to Who??? The Winners, Losers, and WTFs of the Academy Awards.
format: Sean Fennessey and Amanda Dobbins recap the 93rd Academy Awards — Anthony Hopkins over Chadwick Boseman, Best Picture handed out before the acting awards, Soderbergh's freeze-frame ending — then take night-of listener questions.

films.title: The Father
films.note: Anthony Hopkins won Best Actor for it; he didn't attend, so Soderbergh's reordered show froze on a photo of him and ended. Amanda liked the film.

films.title: Ma Rainey's Black Bottom
films.note: Chadwick Boseman was the expected posthumous Best Actor winner; instead it took only costume design and makeup/hairstyling. The hosts wonder if voters just never watched it.

films.title: Nomadland
films.note: Best Picture, plus Best Director for Chloe Zhao (only the second woman to win) and Best Actress for producer-star Frances McDormand, who howled like a wolf.

films.title: Minari
films.note: Youn Yuh-jung won Best Supporting Actress and shot her shot at presenter Brad Pitt, whose Plan B produced the film.

films.title: Judas and the Black Messiah
films.note: Daniel Kaluuya won Best Supporting Actor and gave a speech that thanked his parents for having sex; no clip of the performance aired, which set Sean off.

films.title: Another Round
films.note: Thomas Vinterberg's Best International Feature win; his speech moved Amanda and her husband to tears — a rare billboard for the power of movies.

films.title: Promising Young Woman
films.note: Emerald Fennell's Best Original Screenplay win, handed out early alongside adapted screenplay.

films.title: Mank
films.note: Two Oscars, including Best Cinematography for Erik Messerschmidt — Sean's cinematography flyer that paid off. 'Mank has two Oscars, Citizen Kane has one.'

films.title: Sound of Metal
films.note: A soft-upset win for Film Editing.

films.title: The Trial of the Chicago 7
films.note: The only Best Picture nominee shut out entirely; Aaron Sorkin looked confused during trivia night.

films.title: My Octopus Teacher
films.note: Best Documentary Feature. Amanda's grievance: the winners thanked everyone but never named the octopus, and it beat films she rated higher.

films.title: Time
films.note: Documentary nominee Amanda called one of the year's best.

films.title: Collective
films.note: Documentary nominee Amanda called one of the year's best.

films.title: Crip Camp
films.note: Documentary nominee; 'a beautiful movie.'

films.title: The Mole Agent
films.note: Documentary nominee; Amanda joked it should cross over with The Father.

films.title: Hillbilly Elegy
films.note: Glenn Close's nomination; instead of an Oscar the show had her identify and perform 'Da Butt' during trivia night.

films.title: One Night in Miami
films.note: Its song was the pick both hosts made for Best Original Song; it lost to 'Fight for You' from Judas and the Black Messiah in an upset.

films.title: The Life Ahead
films.note: Diane Warren's nominated song, another expected Best Original Song contender that lost.

films.title: Terminator 2: Judgment Day
films.note: Steven Yeun's on-air testimonial about the movies that formed him — a deeply relatable moment.

films.title: E.T. the Extra-Terrestrial
films.note: Lee Isaac Chung's anecdote about standing the whole way through it as a kid.

films.title: Blade Runner
films.note: Harrison Ford's presenting bit; Sean wanted more of these 'when we made this movie' callbacks.

films.title: Joker
films.note: Made Joaquin Phoenix the reigning Best Actor, so he presented — looking, per Sean, unhappy to be there.

films.title: The Irishman
films.note: The prior year's Netflix Best Picture shutout that Chicago 7 now echoes.

films.title: Citizen Kane
films.note: The one-Oscar punchline to Mank's two.

films.title: West Side Story
films.note: Rita Moreno presented and a clip from Spielberg's forthcoming remake aired; the 1961 film won 10 Oscars, a sweep the hosts think is now impossible.

films.title: Titanic
films.note: Cited in the debate over whether a single film can still dominate every category.

films.title: Avatar
films.note: Part of the generational-megablockbuster lineage Sean traces.

films.title: Avengers: Endgame
films.note: 'Two billion dollars' of humans watched it — the movie that absorbed the culture the way awards films no longer do.

segments.heading: Night-of listener questions
segments.note: the mailbag, run by Bobby Wagner

films.title: Extraction
films.note: Sean's joke answer for Brad Pitt's favorite film of the year.

films.title: Spenser Confidential
films.note: Another gag Brad Pitt 'favorite' — the title neither host could remember.

films.title: Happiest Season
films.note: 'He loves rom-coms' — more Brad Pitt bit.

films.title: Bad Boys for Life
films.note: Part of the running Brad Pitt joke list.

films.title: Trolls World Tour
films.note: 'The most overrepresented movie on The Big Picture.'

films.title: Queen & Slim
films.note: Cited as evidence Kaluuya is interesting even when the movie doesn't work; he's the pick for most future Oscars.

films.title: The Wife
films.note: Glenn Close filmography riff — the one only Sean and Amanda saw.

films.title: The World According to Garp
films.note: Floated as a topic between Kaluuya and Glenn Close.

films.title: Fatal Attraction
films.note: Part of guessing Kaluuya's favorite Glenn Close performances.

films.title: The Natural
films.note: Another Glenn Close title in the guessing game.

films.title: Albert Nobbs
films.note: The Glenn Close joke Amanda talked herself out of, for Glenn's sake.

films.title: House of Gucci
films.note: Sean's way-too-early 2022 Best Picture pick — Lady Gaga, Ridley Scott. Amanda passes.

films.title: Black Hawk Down
films.note: Ridley Scott's last Best Director nomination, a 20-year gap by the time of House of Gucci.

films.title: Clifford the Big Red Dog
films.note: Amanda's flip 2022 non-answer, alongside a nod to the next PTA film.

films.title: Mortal Kombat
films.note: Teased as next week's 'cinematic masterpiece' to close the show.

TV: The Falcon and the Winter Soldier
